tldr: Escalating tensions in the Middle East saw Iran claim attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz amidst continued US strikes.
The US imposed new 50% tariffs on Canada, prompting Canada to declare readiness to intensify trade talks.
A new UN report indicates that global hunger is decreasing but remains above pre-COVID-19 levels.

market.why: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite both registered slight declines, likely influenced by caution surrounding escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which included US strikes on Iran and Houthi threats to shipping. (CNBC)

yield_10y.why: The 10-year Treasury yield increased, a movement experts attribute to concerns arising from the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, with yields having risen since the beginning of the Iran war. (Al Jazeera)

vix.why: The VIX saw a minor decrease, potentially reflecting a slight easing of immediate market volatility concerns following reports that mediators have presented a proposal for a 10-day ceasefire in the Middle East. (CNBC)

tech.summary: Halliday's second generation of smart glasses features a significantly improved display, addressing issues found in its previous iteration and enhancing the user experience. (The Verge)
Innovation in materials science is crucial for advancing next-generation AI technologies, enabling greater processing power, memory, and energy efficiency. (MIT Technology Review)
Sony Music Entertainment has filed a lawsuit against the AI music generator Udio, alleging infringement of copyright for over 30,000 songs. (The Verge)

world.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard claims attacks on an oil tanker and two other ships in the Strait of Hormuz, occurring as the US conducts its tenth consecutive night of strikes in the region. (Guardian World)
The US has imposed 50% tariffs on Canada, escalating trade tensions and prompting Canada to express its readiness to intensify trade discussions to resolve the dispute. (BBC World)
